title: 'Alien: Romulus' $41M+ Scores Record Openings For Fede Alvarez & Cailee Spaeny; 2nd Best For Franchise
text: Fede Álvarez's sci-fi horror film achieved a dominant opening weekend, earning $41.5 million domestically. This marks the second-highest debut in the 45-year Alien franchise's history, trailing only Ridley Scott's 2012 prequel Prometheus, which opened to $51 million. The film expanded globally to $108.2 million, demonstrating strong international appeal. The movie benefited from extensive marketing partnerships, including theater takeovers, custom merchandise like Xenomorph popcorn buckets at major chains, and cross-promotion through Disney's media ecosystem including ESPN, FX, Hulu, and Disney+. Audience reception remained positive, with an 82% Rotten Tomatoes score and strong exit polling showing an "82% positive" audience response. However, the B+ CinemaScore slightly underperformed compared to earlier franchise entries like James Cameron's Aliens, which earned an A rating. The predominantly male audience (70%) skewed toward ages 25-34, with premium formats representing 52% of ticket sales. The opening positioned Disney to maintain box office dominance into early September.
